mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect ongoing labor market expansion that supports aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative on average during months with positive PAYEMS growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.25% reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates short-term borrowing costs and compresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.25% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production combined with an elevated federal funds rate creates a joint tightening of credit conditions and manufacturing slowdown that amplifies downside earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO changes and FEDFUNDS levels fails to predict negative 60-day returns, yielding a walk-forward Rank IC greater than zero over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions with unemployment below 5%, a slowdown in real GDP growth signals diminishing corporate revenue momentum that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 slowdowns during periods with UNRATE below 5% do not coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1800 thousand units reflect robust construction activity and household formation that supports cyclical demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when HOUST exceeds 1800 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive growth in industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization that boosts corporate operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 3-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered observations over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals a deteriorating labor market that precedes declines in consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Increases in UNRATE greater than 0.2 percentage points are not followed by negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation pressures the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which raises nominal discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL annualized changes above 3.5% coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during tightening cycles.
